data.content:
# Implementation Plan: CLI Scope Flags

## Context & Dependencies
Part of the 4-knot prompt-run-ledger scope wave. Sibling knots provide the
substrate this CLI work targets:
- knots-41ba — domain model fields (scope struct on the knot)
- knots-7dda — event log persistence/replay for scope updates
- knots-ee7d — projection of scope into KnotView / `kno show` output
- knots-5091 — this knot; CLI surface

Sequencing: 41ba -> 7dda -> ee7d -> 5091. CLI work cannot land before
41ba's fields exist or there is nothing to store; show output coverage in
verification step 3 depends on ee7d.

## Field / Flag Mapping (from wave spec)
Domain field           -> CLI flag
volume                 -> --scope-volume                  (positive integer)
scale                  -> --scope-scale                   (string, e.g. fib_v1)
volume_score_confidence-> --scope-volume-score-confidence (f64 0.0-1.0)
volume_stddev          -> --scope-volume-stddev           (f64 >= 0.0)
volume_result_id       -> --scope-volume-result-id        (string)
reliability            -> --scope-reliability             (i64 0-100)
reliability_score_confidence -> --scope-reliability-score-confidence (f64 0.0-1.0)
reliability_stddev     -> --scope-reliability-stddev      (f64 >= 0.0)
reliability_band       -> --scope-reliability-band        (string)
reliability_result_id  -> --scope-reliability-result-id   (string)

## Steps
1. Define a shared ScopePatch struct (10 Option fields) next to
   NewOperation/UpdateOperation in src/write_dispatch/operation_map.rs.
   Used by both create and update paths to avoid drift.

2. Extend NewArgs (src/cli.rs, lines 187-256) with the 10 scope flags.
   Each flag: Option<T> with #[arg(long, help = "...")]. Group under
   help_heading = "Scope metadata".

3. Mirror the same 10 flags on UpdateArgs (src/cli_agent.rs, lines
   175-340) with identical types and help text.

4. Extend operation_map.rs:
   - map_new() copies scope flags into NewOperation.scope.
   - map_update() copies scope flags into UpdateOperation.scope.

5. Add validate_scope_patch(&ScopePatch) -> Result<(), AppError> in a new
   src/write_dispatch/execute/scope_validation.rs. Rules:
   - volume == 0          -> "scope_volume must be > 0"
   - reliability outside 0..=100 -> "scope_reliability must be between 0 and 100"
   - any *_score_confidence outside 0.0..=1.0 -> message naming the field
   - any *_stddev < 0.0   -> message naming the field
   Call from execute_new() (src/write_dispatch/execute/mod.rs:58) and
   execute_update() (src/write_dispatch/execute/execute_write_ops.rs:20)
   before invoking the app methods.

6. Plumb scope into the app layer (depends on knots-41ba):
   - Add scope to CreateKnotOptions and the update patch built by
     build_update_patch() (src/app/knot_update/fields.rs).
   - Surface fields in KnotView via knots-ee7d.

7. Tests:
   - src/cli_tests.rs: new_parses_scope_flags() and
     update_parses_scope_flags() patterned after
     update_parses_invariant_flags() (line 209). Cover every flag, value
     types, and a no-flag baseline.
   - src/write_dispatch/tests.rs: integration tests for each validation
     boundary (volume=0, reliability=-1, reliability=101, confidence=1.5,
     confidence=-0.1, stddev=-0.001) and a happy-path round trip.
   - Manual checks match the knot Verification block (kno new -> kno show
     -> kno update -> kno show -> cargo test).

## Validation Strategy
Post-parse, follow the existing priority validation pattern
(src/app/knot_update/fields.rs:125-147). Clap value_parser is not used
elsewhere; staying consistent. Error messages name the CLI-facing field
(e.g. "scope_volume") so users can fix the offending flag.

## Help Text
Each flag's help string states unit and range:
- "Scope volume estimate (positive integer)."
- "Reliability score (0-100 integer)."
- "Volume score confidence (0.0 to 1.0)."
- "Volume stddev (non-negative float)."
etc. Cover ranges so users see them in --help without reading docs.

## Risks
- Sibling sequencing: implementation cannot start until 41ba lands. Flag
  the dependency on the implementation step and revisit if siblings stall.
- scope_scale enumeration: wave only shows fib_v1. Keep CLI permissive
  (free-form string); let domain layer enforce the closed set later.
- Coverage ratchet (95%): scope_validation.rs must be fully covered;
  every error branch needs a test.

## Out of Scope
- No model changes here (owned by 41ba).
- No event log changes (owned by 7dda).
- No KnotView changes (owned by ee7d).
- No bulk import / JSON ingest of scope (not required by knot
  verification).
